items.headline: Mauritius weather bulletin – 03 April 2026
items.summary: STS INDUSA|Heavy Rain Watch for Mauritius Weather news for Mauritius issued at 17h00 on Friday 03 April 2026.General situation:1. Heavy Rain Watch is in force in Mauritius till 10h00 tomorrow, Saturday 04 April 2026.2. Clouds associated with the frontal system coming from the West, will influence the local weather at times.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Rather cloudy tonight and tomorrow morning with showers, moderate at times. These showers may be temporarily heavy and accompanied by thunderstorms.There may be water accumulations in flood prone areas.Visibility will be reduced due to fog and in showers.The minimum temperature will vary between 19 and 21 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 23 and 25 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Partly cloudy in the afternoon becoming cloudy at times over the highgrounds and over windward slopes with a few showers.The maximum temperature will vary between 24 and 27 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 28 and 31 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind from North-west at about 15 km/h, blowing from South at about 20 km/h tomorrow.Sea rough beyond the reefs with South-westerly swells of 2 metres 50.Ventures in the open sea are not advised.The public is advised to:1. remain in safe places and avoid open areas, hikings, sea ventures and sheltering under trees during thundery showers.2. avoid places prone to water accumulation.3. be very cautious on the roadsSupplementary Information:At 16h00, the severe tropical storm INDUSA was centred near the point 16.5 degrees south latitude and 72.0 degrees east longitude. It is moving in a general south-southwesterly direction at around 15 km/h and will not directly influence the weather over the Mascarene Islands.High tides :Tomorrow 01h42 and 14h19.Low tides :19h48 and tomorrow 08h21.Sunset :18h09.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h18.Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1009 hectopascals.

The FRANCE 24 Observers team worked with an international media consortium coordinated by Forbidden Stories to analyse these leaked documents, which show just how susceptible these media outlets are to foreign interference.

When the pan-African media outlet The Continent received a trove of leaked documents about this operation, the FRANCE 24 Observers team joined forces with a consortium of international journalists to investigate the inner workings of this network.
